---
title: "Julia Gong"
image: headshot/headshot.jpg
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/julia-gong-b8222830a
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/juliacygong/E155
toc: false
---

My name is Julia Gong, and I an engineering student at Harvey Mudd College and a Clay Wolkin Research Fellow (co 2027). My research focuses on low-level computer architecture and processor design, including implementing the hypervisor extension for a RISC-V processor and developing verification tests across machine, supervisor, and user privilege modes. This fall, I will continue this work by designing and implementing a superscalar RISC-V processor to explore higher-performance execution and instruction-level parallelism.

I did not come into college expecting to focus on hardware, but after taking a Digital Design and Computer Architecture course I found myself drawn to the logic and structure of how computers function from gate-level circuits to pipelined processors. I have always enjoyed creative, hands-on work — including pottery, embroidery, and beadwork — so I really enjoyed the creative process of designing finite state machines and building systems that interact on the microarchitectural level. I am interested in work that brings together low-level systems thinking with thoughtful design, and I'm continuing to explore where that intersection may lead!

This site includes my technical work from E155 (Microprocessor System Design & Applications) as well as some of my artwork. You can find all my E155 source code and research contributions on [GitHub](https://github.com/juliacygong/E155).

Feel free to reach out to me at jgong@g.hmc.edu or using my Linkedin!
